clusters.title: Early takeaways from videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's Epstein depositions

items.title: Early takeaways from videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's Epstein depositions
items.standfirst: The House Oversight Committee has released videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's depositions on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.
items.content: The House Oversight Committee has released videos of Bill and Hillary Clinton's depositions on convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.

items.title: Video of Clintons' Epstein testimony released by House committee
items.standfirst: The House Oversight Committee released recordings of last week's depositions with former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.
items.content: The House Oversight Committee released recordings of last week's depositions with former President Bill Clinton and former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton.

items.title: US House committee releases videos of the Clintons’ Epstein testimonies
items.standfirst: Bill and Hillary Clinton last week faced hours of questioning from lawmakers about the convicted sex offenderSign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inboxVideos of Bill Clinton, the former US president, and Hillary Clinton, the former US secretary of state, answering questions about convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein were released on Monday by a House committee investigating the late financier.The recordings of the depositions, which spanned hours over two days last week, show how both Clintons distanced themselves from Epstein. Bill Clinton told the committee that he had ended his relationship with Epstein years before the financier entered a guilty plea in 2008 to soliciting prostitution from an underage girl. Continue reading...

Bill Clinton told the committee that he had ended his relationship with Epstein years before the financier entered a guilty plea in 2008 to soliciting prostitution from an underage girl. Continue reading...

CBS News legal reporter Katrina Kaufman has more.

items.title: Video of Clinton depositions in Epstein investigation released by House Republicans
items.standfirst: Over hours of testimony, the Clintons both denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes prior to his pleading guilty in 2008 to state charges in Florida for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl.
items.content: Over hours of testimony, the Clintons both denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes prior to his pleading guilty in 2008 to state charges in Florida for soliciting prostitution from an underage girl.(Image credit: Melina Mara)

clusters.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal, could deploy atomic weapons to European allies

items.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal, could deploy atomic weapons to European allies
items.standfirst: President Emmanuel Macron said Monday that France will expand its atomic arsenal and could deploy nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries for the first time, as he unveiled an unprecedented plan to bolster security on the continent. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: President Emmanuel Macron said Monday that France will expand its atomic arsenal and could deploy nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries for the first time, as he unveiled an unprecedented plan to bolster security on the continent. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: France to increase nuclear arsenal and European weapons cooperation, Macron says
items.standfirst: French president says Paris could deploy nuclear-capable fighter jet to countries such as Germany and PolandFrance will increase the size of its nuclear arsenal for the first time in decades and significantly intensify nuclear weapons cooperation with eight European allies including the UK as part of a “major” strengthening of its deterrence doctrine, Emmanuel Macron has said.Amid growing concern among European leaders about wavering US commitments to help defend the continent, the French president said on Monday that Paris could deploy nuclear-capable Rafale fighter jets to partner countries such as Germany and Poland. Continue reading...
items.content: French president says Paris could deploy nuclear-capable fighter jet to countries such as Germany and PolandFrance will increase the size of its nuclear arsenal for the first time in decades and significantly intensify nuclear weapons cooperation with eight European allies including the UK as part of a “major” strengthening of its deterrence doctrine, Emmanuel Macron has said.Amid growing concern among European leaders about wavering US commitments to help defend the continent, the French president said on Monday that Paris could deploy nuclear-capable Rafale fighter jets to partner countries such as Germany and Poland. Continue reading...

items.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal and extend deterrence to European allies
items.standfirst: Emmanuel Macron said eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.
items.content: Emmanuel Macron said eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.

items.title: Macron says France will allow temporary deployment of nuclear-armed jets to allied nations
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that France would allow the deployment of nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries as part of a new nuclear strategy.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron announced that France would allow the deployment of nuclear-armed aircraft to allied countries as part of a new nuclear strategy.

clusters.title: Inside Trump's thinking on war with Iran

items.title: Inside Trump's thinking on war with Iran
items.standfirst: President Trump has said the conflict with Iran is projected to last from four to five weeks, but has the capacity to go on for longer. CBS News chief Washington analyst Robert Costa joins to discuss what he's learned about the president's mindset after speaking with him.
items.content: President Trump has said the conflict with Iran is projected to last from four to five weeks, but has the capacity to go on for longer. CBS News chief Washington analyst Robert Costa joins to discuss what he's learned about the president's mindset after speaking with him.

items.title: Trump says Iran war to last four to five weeks but could go ‘far longer’
items.standfirst: President says he ordered attacks to thwart Tehran nuclear aims – and abruptly pivots to talk up White House ballroomUS-Israel war on Iran – live updatesDonald Trump has laid out four goals in Iran and said the US campaign had been projected to last four to five weeks but could “go far longer than that”.On Monday, the US president offered his most extensive comments yet about the war, going beyond two video messages and a series of brief phone interviews with reporters that offered sometimes conflicting objectives. Continue reading...
items.content: President says he ordered attacks to thwart Tehran nuclear aims – and abruptly pivots to talk up White House ballroomUS-Israel war on Iran – live updatesDonald Trump has laid out four goals in Iran and said the US campaign had been projected to last four to five weeks but could “go far longer than that”.On Monday, the US president offered his most extensive comments yet about the war, going beyond two video messages and a series of brief phone interviews with reporters that offered sometimes conflicting objectives. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Whatever it takes': Trump says the Iran conflict will continue
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump laid out a potential timeline of the Iran conflict saying, "we projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that." The president also said Iran had to be stopped from creating nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles for the safety of U.S. citizens.
items.content: President Donald Trump laid out a potential timeline of the Iran conflict saying, "we projected four to five weeks, but we have capability to go far longer than that." The president also said Iran had to be stopped from creating nuclear weapons and ballistic missiles for the safety of U.S. citizens.

ai_summary: Trump expects conflict to last four to five weeks
Conflict could go on for longer than expected
US aims to stop Iran's nuclear program
Trump says US has capability to go far longer
Conflict goals include preventing nuclear weapons

clusters.title: Some Middle East flights resume, but thousands of travelers are still stranded by war

items.title: Some Middle East flights resume, but thousands of travelers are still stranded by war
items.standfirst: Limited flights out of the Middle East resumed on Monday. But hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded in the region after attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel.
items.content: Limited flights out of the Middle East resumed on Monday. But hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded in the region after attacks on Iran by the U.S. and Israel.(Image credit: Fadel Senna)

items.title: War in the Middle East: Thousands of flights cancelled… across the globe
items.standfirst: ✈️❌ Thousands of flights have been canceled around the world on the third day of war in the Middle East. ➡️ Australia, Indonesia, and Vietnam... hundreds of thousands of travelers are still stranded. #MiddleEast #Iran #UnitedStates #Israel #Plane #Aviation

items.title: Travel chaos intensifies with airports closed, flights canceled amid Iran war

items.title: Prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure would cause oil prices to surge, experts warn
items.standfirst: The Iran war is renewing concerns about the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. A prolonged closure could sharply drive up oil prices, experts said.
items.content: The Iran war is renewing concerns about the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz. A prolonged closure could sharply drive up oil prices, experts said.

items.title: Oil prices surge, but no panic yet, as Iran war continues
items.standfirst: Global oil prices are in the high $70s as traffic through Strait of Hormuz comes to a halt. Some analysts have warned they could top $100 a barrel if the stoppage is prolonged.
items.content: Global oil prices are in the high $70s as traffic through Strait of Hormuz comes to a halt. Some analysts have warned they could top $100 a barrel if the stoppage is prolonged.(Image credit: Fadel Senna)

items.title: Starmer criticises Trump, saying UK 'does not believe in regime change from skies'
items.standfirst: The PM told MPs that Trump had expressed his disagreement with the decision not to get involved in the initial strikes.

items.title: ‘Duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest’: Starmer defends UK position on Iran
items.standfirst: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has pushed back after criticism from US President Trump over Britain's decision to stay out of the initial US and Israeli strikes against Iran. It "is my duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest", Starmer told parliament on Monday.
items.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has pushed back after criticism from US President Trump over Britain's decision to stay out of the initial US and Israeli strikes against Iran. It "is my duty to judge what is in Britain's national interest", Starmer told parliament on Monday.

items.title: "There is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't get involved in Iran"
items.standfirst: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer on ​March 2nd said he stood by his decision not ​to ‌support initial joint ⁠U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump had ⁠criticised his approach. But "there is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't be getting involved", says FRANCE 24's Philip Turle.
items.content: British Prime ​Minister Keir Starmer on ​March 2nd said he stood by his decision not ​to ‌support initial joint ⁠U.S.-Israeli strikes against Iran. Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump had ⁠criticised his approach. But "there is a universal feeling the UK shouldn't be getting involved", says FRANCE 24's Philip Turle.
